back_translation: As soon as dawn came, the chief priests, together with the elders and scribes, and the whole Sanhedrin council, consulted together, then bound Jesus (royal) and took him (royal) to hand over to Pilate.
Pilate then asked him (royal), 'Are you the king of the Jews?' He (royal) answered him, 'You have said so.'
The chief priests then all proceeded to accuse him (royal) of many things.
Pilate questioned him (royal) again, 'Do you not answer anything at all? See how much they are accusing you of.'
But Jesus (royal) did not speak to answer anything further. So Pilate was amazed.
At that festival, Pilate was accustomed to release a prisoner to them according to what they requested.
There was one man named Barabbas, who was imprisoned together with the rebels who had committed murder in the insurrection.
The crowd went up and began to request Pilate to do as he had usually done for them.
Pilate answered them, 'Do you (pl.) want us to release the king of the Jews to you (pl.)?'
Because he knew that the chief priests had handed him (royal) over out of envy.
But the chief priests incited the crowd so that Pilate would release Barabbas instead.
Pilate then answered them again, 'If so, what do you (pl.) want us to do with the one whom you (pl.) call the king of the Jews?'
They cried out again, 'Nail him to the cross!'
Pilate then asked them, 'Why then? What evil has he done?' But they cried out all the more strongly, 'Nail him to the cross!'
Pilate, wishing to please the crowd, released Barabbas to them, and after having Jesus flogged, handed him (royal) over to be crucified.
The soldiers led him (royal) into the palace-courtyard, which is the Praetorium, and summoned the whole military company together.
They put a purple robe on him (royal), and wove a crown of thorns and set it on his (royal) head.
Then they began to salute him (royal), 'Long live His Majesty, King of the Jews!'
They took a reed and struck his (royal) head, spat on him (royal), and kneeling down, bowed before him (royal) mockingly.
When they had finished mocking him (royal), they removed the purple robe and put his (royal) own clothes on him, then led him (royal) out to crucify him at the cross.
The soldiers conscripted a man who was passing by, named Simon, a Cyrenean, father of Alexander and Rufus, who came from the country, to carry his (royal) cross.
They brought him (royal) to a place called Golgotha, which means 'Place of the Skull.'
They gave him (royal) wine mixed with myrrh to drink (royal), but he (royal) did not receive it.
And they nailed him (royal) to the cross, and divided his (royal) clothes by casting lots to see who would take what.
It was about nine in the morning when they nailed him (royal) to the cross.
The sign stating his (royal) charge that was inscribed read, 'The King of the Jews.'
They crucified two robbers together with him (royal), one on his right side and the other on his left side.
Those who passed by reviled him (royal), shaking their heads and saying, 'Aha! You who are going to destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days,
Save yourself and come down from the cross!'
Likewise, the chief priests and scribes also mocked him (royal), saying among themselves, 'He could save others, but he cannot save himself.
Let the Christ, the king of Israel, come down from the cross now, that we may see and believe.' Even those who were crucified with him (royal) also reviled him (royal).
When noon came, darkness covered the whole land until three in the afternoon.
At three in the afternoon, Jesus (royal) cried out with a loud voice, 'Eloi, Eloi, lema sabachthani,' which means, 'My God, my God, why have you (royal) forsaken me (humble)?'
Some of those who stood there, when they heard this, said, 'Look, he is calling Elijah.'

One man ran to take a sponge dipped in sour wine and fixed it on the end of a reed and offered it to him (royal) to drink (royal), saying, 'Leave him alone. Let us see whether Elijah will come to take him down.'
But Jesus (royal) uttered a loud voice and then expired (royal-register for death).
Then the curtain in the temple was torn in two, from top to bottom.
When the centurion who stood opposite him (royal) saw that he (royal) had expired (royal) in this manner, he said, 'Truly, this man was the Son of God.'
There were also some women watching from a distance, among whom were Mary of Magdala, Mary the mother of James the younger and of Joses, and Salome,
who used to follow and minister to him (royal) when he (royal) was in the region of Galilee, and there were also many other women who had come up to the city of Jerusalem with him (royal).
When evening had already come, because it was the day of preparation — that is, the day before the sabbath —
Joseph of Arimathea, an honorable member of the council, who himself also was waiting for the kingdom of God, boldly went in to Pilate and asked for the body (royal) of Jesus.
Pilate was amazed that he (royal) had expired (royal) so quickly, so he summoned the centurion and asked him whether he (royal) had been dead (royal) for long.
When he had learned from the centurion, he granted the body (royal) to Joseph.

Joseph bought a linen cloth, then took him (royal) down from the cross, wrapped the body (royal) with that linen cloth, took it to lay in a tomb which had been hewn out of rock, and rolled a stone to close the mouth of the tomb.
As for Mary of Magdala and Mary the mother of Joses, they kept watching to see where he (royal) was placed.
